item.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off with Shakira at 1st of 3 opening ceremonies
item.description: With matches being played in 11 cities across the U.S., Mexico and Canada​, fans are getting three World Cup opening ceremonies.

item.title: When will the Iran war end? Here's what Trump has said.
item.description: President Trump has offered timelines of days and weeks for the Iran war, but a solution remains elusive.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Trump says "settlement" reached on Iran, signing could happen this weekend
item.description: President Trump said earlier Thursday he called off new military strikes on Iran, hours after threatening to escalate the war.

item.title: World Cup kicks off with match in Mexico
item.description: The Men's World Cup officially kicked off Thursday with a match between Mexico and South Africa in Mexico City. Billions of fans are expected to watch the tournament worldwide. CBS News correspondent Nicole Valdes reports on fans' excitement.

item.title: Wholesale prices see biggest spike since 2022 as energy costs climb
item.description: The producer price index came in much higher than expected in May, signaling that U.S. consumers may face more inflation in the coming months. CBS News' Kelly O'Grady explains.

item.title: U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding likely to be signed next week
item.description: The signing of the memorandum or letter of intent would kick off 60 days of talks to negotiate details of an enduring U.S.-Iran agreement.

item.title: Markings that appear to show "86 47" seen on National Mall
item.description: A photographer from the Reuters news outlet saw the apparent "86 47" markings from atop the Washington Monument.

item.title: Relative of suspect in Anna Kepner's killing calls for her parents to face charges
item.description: Timothy Hudson, 16, is accused of sexually assaulting and killing Anna Kepner, his 18-year-old stepsister, while the family was on a cruise.

item.title: Relative of teen charged in Anna Kepner killing says Kepner's father, stepmother should face charges
item.description: The step-grandmother of Timothy Hudson, the 16-year-old stepbrother of Anna Kepner, who has been charged in her death and sexual assault, says Kepner's father and stepmother should be charged with parental neglect.

item.title: Trump taps prosecutor Jay Clayton as next director of national intelligence
item.description: Jay Clayton is currently the U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York.

item.title: Trump takes questions on potential Iran peace deal progress after canceling strikes
item.description: President Trump took questions from reporters on Thursday after saying he's canceling planned strikes on Iran, citing progress on a potential peace deal.

item.title: Alabama inmate's nitrogen gas execution tonight hinges on last-minute appeal
item.description: The Alabama Attorney General's Office petitioned the Supreme Court to reverse a federal judge's decision to permanently ban the state from putting Jeffrey Lee to death using nitrogen hypoxia.

item.title: Trump speaks about decision to cancel planned Iran strikes
item.description: President Trump on Thursday announced he was canceling planned strikes against Iran, citing progress on a potential peace deal. Mr. Trump spoke about the decision from the Oval Office.

item.title: The 2026 World Cup begins today. Here's what to know.
item.description: Charlie Davies, a former player for the U.S. Men's National Team, joins CBS News with more insight as the 2026 FIFA World Cup begins.

item.title: $40,000 6-month CD vs. $40,000 1-year CD: Which will earn more interest now?
item.description: Don't deposit $40,000 into either CD account term before knowing the interest earnings each offers right now.

item.title: Details on progress toward Iran peace agreement as Trump calls off strikes
item.description: President Trump on Thursday announced he would cancel all planned strikes against Iran and said progress was being made on a potential peace deal. CBS News' Margaret Brennan and Courtney Kealy have more.

item.title: World Cup opening ceremony kicks off in Mexico with Shakira
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup opening ceremony festivities got underway a little over an hour before the first kickoff in Mexico City's Estadio Azteca.

item.title: Trump taps federal prosecutor Jay Clayton for director of national intelligence role
item.description: President Trump on Thursday announced he's nominating former SEC chairman and current U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York Jay Clayton to be the next director of national intelligence. CBS News congressional correspondent Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: SpaceX IPO could see a "volatile few hours" after launch, market reporter says
item.description: SpaceX goes public on Friday, with its initial public offering poised to make waves. Joe Ciolli, executive editor of the First Trade Newsletter at Business Insider, shares his market performance predictions for Elon Musk's aerospace company.

item.title: U.S. charges 3 for alleged smuggling conspiracy involving unaccompanied minors
item.description: The Justice Department announced it has charged three people in Ohio for conspiring to smuggle unaccompanied minors across the U.S. border.

item.title: The World Cup will draw millions of fans, but economic payoff is modest
item.description: Although the five-week soccer tournament starting on Thursday is the largest sporting event ever, the U.S. economic gains are likely to be muted.

item.title: Supreme Court hands down opinions on criminal trial venues, bankruptcy court, investment regulations
item.description: The Supreme Court issued new opinions in three court cases on Thursday. A series of major rulings remain as the end of the court's current term nears. CBS News chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford explains.

item.title: False alarm apparently triggered hazmat response at Pentagon, sources say
item.description: Multiple floors of the Pentagon were locked down for several hours Thursday morning and hazmat crews were deployed for what authorities had described as a "hazardous materials incident."

item.title: Is a high-yield savings account worth opening with inflation rising now?
item.description: Inflation is rising yet again, but is a high-yield savings account worth opening in response? Here's why it may be.

item.title: U.S. monitoring Chinese activity in South China Sea around disputed shoal
item.description: Analysts are scrutinizing recent Chinese maritime operations near Scarborough Shoal, an uninhabited but strategically located atoll near the Philippine island of Luzon, U.S. officials said.

item.title: U.S. cruise passengers accused of assaulting police in Bahamas
item.description: Four police officers were injured, including one who was taken to the hospital, authorities said.

item.title: See when the U.S. men's soccer team is playing in 2026 World Cup
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team, which last appeared at the 2022 World Cup, will face Paraguay to kick off its 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Human remains found in sleeping bag in national park identified 26 years later
item.description: Forensic tests helped identify a man whose remains were found inside a sleeping bag in Washington state in 2000.

item.title: In a tough economy, many U.S. teachers forced to work second jobs
item.description: According to a recent survey, 71% of U.S. public school teachers said they work at least one second job.
